intelligence.headline: South Africa advances Article 6.2 bilateral framework - ITMO eligibility criteria published
intelligence.summary: South Africa's Department of Forestry, Fisheries and the Environment has published formal eligibility criteria for Internationally Transferred Mitigation Outcomes under Article 6.2. The criteria confirm ARR methodology VM0047 as a qualifying activity, with corresponding adjustments required from project developers. This is a material development for South African carbon projects seeking compliance-grade offtake, including the Save the Sand pipeline in Mpumalanga.

intelligence.headline: Bezos Earth Fund opens $200M African biodiversity round - applications open May 2026
intelligence.summary: The Bezos Earth Fund has announced a $200M funding round focused on African biodiversity, with applications opening May 2026. The round targets landscape-scale projects with demonstrable community co-governance structures and third-party certification. Grants are expected to range from $5M to $40M with a 5-year commitment horizon. This is one of the largest single philanthropic allocations to African conservation in the current cycle.

intelligence.headline: World Bank approves $500M debt-for-nature framework for Sub-Saharan Africa
intelligence.summary: The World Bank has approved a $500M debt-for-nature swap framework targeting Sub-Saharan Africa, with Kenya and Zambia confirmed as pilot countries. The mechanism allows eligible sovereigns to retire a portion of external debt in exchange for ring-fenced conservation spending commitments. Structuring is being led by the IFC with Conservation Finance Alliance as technical advisor. Further country applications are expected in Q3 2026.

intelligence.headline: New meta-analysis confirms community-managed lands match state protected areas on biodiversity outcomes
intelligence.summary: A meta-analysis published in Nature Sustainability covering 600 sites across 40 countries found that community-managed lands perform equivalently to state protected areas on key biodiversity outcome measures, at 60% lower per-hectare cost. The findings challenge the dominant NGO-led, state-backed conservation model and add significant weight to community co-ownership structures as a financing and governance standard. CCB Gold certification was identified as the strongest proxy indicator for community governance quality.
